title: Meta Ads Designer Needed for 3 Parenting Retargeting Static Creatives

evidence: images 2-7 each show a mother/teen pair in domestic settings matching all 3 concept photo briefs precisely
tt_logo_stamp (image 1) is purple round stamp exactly as brief mandates for top-corner placement
deductions: photos_tall[2] / photos_square[2] (images 4,7) show a white-haired older mother — concept 3 specifies 40s mother, this reads late 50s
no asset covers the brief's explicit requirement for English-speaking / diverse audience breadth across all 3 concepts — concepts 1 & 2 are diverse, concept 3 is not
All images depict mothers and teens in emotional scenarios matching the brief.
Logo (image 1) is the specified TeenTalk purple round stamp.
Mother/teen imagery matches each concept's photo_brief
Logo stamp (image 1) matches brand requirement

evidence: ad_copy.json supplies all 3 concept headlines, supporting lines, CTAs, and photo briefs — full copy coverage
brand_guide.md supplies hex palette, stamp rules, type hierarchy, and do/don't list
deductions: creative copy is marked 'provided after hiring' in brief but IS present in ad_copy.json — no actual gap, but the brief's own framing creates ambiguity an executor must resolve
no transparent-background version of tt_logo_stamp supplied; asset_add_file step needs PNG with alpha to overlay cleanly on photos
All required creative copy, brand guidelines, logo, and imagery are present.
Six distinct mother/teen images (tall and square pairs) are provided for the three concepts.
All 3 photo_briefs have matching tall and square imagery
All required brand, copy, and visual assets present

fix.problem: Logo stamp has white background fill — overlay on photo will show white square halo
fix.prompt_delta: Regenerate as PNG with fully transparent background; purple ring and wordmark only, no white fill, no drop shadow, 1024x1024 px

evidence: photos_tall dims 1024x1536 are narrower than target 1080x1920 — upscale of ~5% needed before crop, minor but real agent step
tt_logo_stamp has opaque white background (image 1 visible) — asset_add_file overlay will produce white halo on every creative
deductions: stamp white background blocks clean compositing in asset_add_file step without an extra remove-background call not in the stated workflow
photos_square at 1024x1024 must be upscaled to 1080x1080 before image_crop_and_resize — small but adds an unlisted step
Image dimensions (1024x1536, 1024x1024) are suitable for cropping to target aspect ratios.
Ad copy provides clear headline, supporting line, and CTA for text filling.
Tall and square images allow cropping for all formats
Logo stamp is isolated and high-res for overlay

fix.problem: Opaque white fill blocks asset_add_file overlay; workflow has no background-removal step for the stamp
fix.prompt_delta: Output as PNG32 with transparent background; only the purple circle ring and TeenTalk wordmark rendered, alpha channel preserved, 1024x1024 px

evidence: images 2 and 5 (concept 1 kitchen/phone scene) are indistinguishable from editorial stock — candid framing, natural light, authentic wariness
images 3 and 6 (concept 2 sofa/headphones) have correct mood and warm lamp atmosphere matching brief
deductions: photos_tall[2] (image 4) feels slightly too polished/editorial for the 'real, not staged' directive — pristine kitchen, perfect composition
photos_square[2] (image 7) similarly reads as high-end lifestyle shoot rather than candid domestic moment
Images portray realistic, un-staged emotional interactions between mothers and teens.
Moods like 'weary, reflective' and 'distant, observant' are authentically captured.
Imagery is candid, documentary, not staged or clinical
Logo stamp is plausible as a supplied asset

evidence: brand_guide.md and brand/persona block agree on all five hex values and both font names — no contradiction
tt_logo_stamp colour (#6B4FA1 purple) matches Brand Purple in palette exactly
deductions: photos span three distinct ethnicities across concepts — visually coherent as a diverse campaign set, but concept 3 demographic shift (older white mother) breaks the 40s-mother consistency thread
no font specimen asset supplied — executor must trust brand_guide.md spec without visual confirmation
Logo color matches brand guide's purple.
Brand guide defines consistent palette, fonts, and voice for all elements.
Image selection and copy align with the 'emotionally intelligent, serious-warm' brand voice.
All assets use consistent palette and brand voice

evidence: assumption to skip Adobe Stock licensing and use generated photography is reasonable given commercial-clearance equivalence and scene-control benefit
two-frame-per-concept input strategy (tall + square) to cover three output ratios is sound and well-justified
deductions: ad_copy.json is recorded as 'provided after hiring' in brief inputs yet is fully present — this real input is wrongly framed as a future deliverable, creating executor confusion about whether copy is final
stamp transparency issue is not flagged anywhere in decisions — a foreseeable blocker left unaddressed
Decision to provide tall and square images for crop latitude is sound and efficient.
Decision to use generated photography as stock stand-in ensures commercial clearance and control.
Assumptions about cropping and generated stock are justified
No real input wrongly demoted to assumption

fix.problem: Decision log misclassifies present copy as future input, risking executor treating it as placeholder
fix.prompt_delta: Update decision record: ad_copy.json is CONFIRMED FINAL client-approved copy for all 3 concepts; executor should treat all fields as locked and proceed without awaiting further copy delivery

top_risks: CLIENT-REJECT (sev 8): The actual creative copy and directional brief for all 3 ads is explicitly stated as 'provided after hiring' — meaning the single most critical input needed to begin design work is missing at handoff. I cannot approve production starting on placeholder assets when the headlines, supporting lines, and CTA text that drive every layout decision haven't been delivered yet.

AGENT CHOKE on 'ad_copy': Although ad_copy.json appears populated in the inventory, the original brief repeatedly states copy is 'provided after hiring,' creating a contractual ambiguity about whether this JSON is final and approved. The agent will treat it as provisional or stall awaiting client confirmation before fill_text can execute, blocking all 9 downstream file outputs.
